insight: Two consecutive days (07-08, 07-09) failed with the identical "No route available" swap error for cirBTC, indicating a persistent routing/liquidity problem for this token pair rather than a transient glitch — the allocation logic itself is fine and not the bottleneck.
patterns: Repeated error_swap_failed on 2 of last 2 attempted swaps, both with 'No route available' for cirBTC
Allocation sizing (1.00 USDC, daily cap) is consistently correct and passes guardrails cleanly - not a clamping issue
Wallet balance keeps growing unspent (19.84 -> 29.84 -> 39.84) because swaps keep failing, so USDC is piling up idle
One earlier error_config on 07-07 suggests possible intermittent config/integration instability with the swap execution layer
strategyAdjustment: Stop retrying the same swap route blindly each day. Before next run, check if cirBTC route/liquidity is available at all (via a route-check or quote call) before committing to a swap attempt; if no route exists, either switch tokenOut to a supported/liquid alternative, reduce trade size to test if smaller amounts route successfully, or flag for manual intervention instead of repeating the same failing action. Also consider accumulating a larger single swap once route is restored, since idle USDC balance is growing unused.
